skillName: Generate geometric sequences using term to term rules
misconceptionSummary: Students can generate terms but do not connect geometric generation to repeated multiplicative growth.

visuals.ruleText: A geometric term-to-term rule multiplies the previous term by a constant
visuals.subText: Each new term is scaled from the one before

steps.narration: A geometric term-to-term rule creates multiplicative growth.
The multiplier tells you how the term is scaled.

lines.text: Because each term depends on the previous one, the numbers can grow very quickly

visuals.label: Each term is a multiplied version of the last

steps.narration: The conceptual key is repeated multiplicative scaling.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: describing a geometric rule as if it adds the same amount. The amount changes, but the multiplier stays fixed.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Focus on how the term is scaled each time, not on the changing size of the jumps.
